perturbation.original_text: The White Hat Parties shall appear in person or by proxy for quorum purposes at the 2020 Annual Meeting, including any adjournment or postponement thereof, to vote all of the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties on the record date (if the 2020 Annual Meeting is within thirty (30) days of the anniversary of the Company's 2019 Annual Meeting of Stockholders, such number of shares shall be no less than 90% of the Current Position) for such meeting (i) in favor of all persons nominated by the Board to serve as directors of the Company and against any stockholder nominated candidate not endorsed by the Board, (ii) against the stockholder proposal made pursuant to Rule 14a-8 under the Exchange Act as disclosed to the White Hat Parties prior to the execution of this Agreement, (iii) to ratify the appointment of the Company's independent registered public accounting firm, and (iv) in accordance with the Board's recommendation with respect to the Company's "say-on-pay" proposal and new equity compensation plan (collectively, the "2020 Proposals"), provided, that with respect to the proposals described in clauses (ii) through (iv) of this Section 4, the White Hat Parties may vote in accordance with the recommendation of Institutional Shareholders Services to the extent such recommendation differs from the voting commitments set forth herein and provided that the White Hat Parties do not publicly disclose their vote on such proposals if it differs from the Board's recommendation; and provided, further, that White Hat Parties shall have the right to vote the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties in their sole discretion with respect to all other proposals brought before the 2020 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.changed_text: The White Hat Parties shall endeavor to appear in person or by proxy for quorum purposes at the 2020 Annual Meeting, including any adjournment or postponement thereof, and may vote all of the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties on the record date (if the 2020 Annual Meeting is within thirty (30) days of the anniversary of the Company's 2019 Annual Meeting of Stockholders, such number of shares shall be no less than 90% of the Current Position) for such meeting (i) in favor of all persons nominated by the Board to serve as directors of the Company and against any stockholder nominated candidate not endorsed by the Board, (ii) against the stockholder proposal made pursuant to Rule 14a-8 under the Exchange Act as disclosed to the White Hat Parties prior to the execution of this Agreement, (iii) to ratify the appointment of the Company's independent registered public accounting firm, and (iv) in accordance with the Board's recommendation with respect to the Company's "say-on-pay" proposal and new equity compensation plan (collectively, the "2020 Proposals"), provided, that with respect to the proposals described in clauses (ii) through (iv) of this Section 4, the White Hat Parties may consider voting in accordance with the recommendation of Institutional Shareholders Services to the extent such recommendation differs from the voting commitments set forth herein and provided that the White Hat Parties do not publicly disclose their vote on such proposals if it differs from the Board's recommendation; and provided, further, that White Hat Parties shall have the right to vote the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties in their sole discretion with respect to all other proposals brought before the 2020 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall appear' to 'shall endeavor to appear' weakens the obligation to attend the meeting for quorum purposes. Changing 'shall vote' to 'may vote' makes the voting commitment non-binding. Furthermore, changing 'may vote in accordance with the recommendation' to 'may consider voting in accordance with the recommendation' introduces ambiguity as it does not guarantee that they will vote, and makes it conditional. Therefore, it is no longer strictly in compliance with the board's nominations and recommendations as originally outlined in the agreement, thus giving the parties a means of non-compliance while technically adhering to the clause.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Section 212(b) of the Delaware General Corporation Law
perturbation.law_explanation: Delaware law requires a clear mechanism for voting shares, whether in person or by proxy, to ensure corporate governance. By weakening the obligation to vote as agreed, the modified clause undermines the purpose of a voting agreement, which is to bind the parties to a specific voting outcome, and contradicts the shareholder's responsibility to exercise their rights in a clear and decisive manner, disrupting the mechanism of the voting shares.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Delaware Code Online Title 8 > Chapter 1 Authenticated PDF Â§ 211 Â§ 212 Â§ 213 Â§ 214 Â§ 215 Â§ 216 Â§ 217 Â§ 218 Â§ 219 Â§ 220 Â§ 221 Â§ 222 Â§ 223 Â§ 224 Â§ 225 Â§ 226 Â§ 227 Â§ 228 Â§ 229 Â§ 230 Â§ 231 Â§ 232 Â§ 233 TITLE 8 Corporations CHAPTER 1. General Corporation Law Subchapter VII. Meetings, Elections, Voting and Notice Â§ 211. Meetings of stockholders. (a) (1) Meetings of stockholders may be held at such place, either within or without this State as may be designated by or in the manner provided in the certificate of incorporation or bylaws, or if not so designated, as determined by the board of directors. If, pursuant to this paragraph or the certificate of incorporation or the bylaws of the corporation, the board of directors is authorized to determine the place of a meeting of stockholders, the board of directors may, in its sole discretion, determine that the meeting shall not be held at any place, but may instead be held solely by means of remote communication as authorized by paragraph (a)(2) of this section. (2) If authorized by the board of directors in its sole discretion, and subject to such guidelines and procedures as the board of directors may adopt, stockholders and proxyholders not physically present at a meeting of stockholders may, by means of remote communication: a. Participate in a meeting of stockholders; and b. Be deemed present in person and vote at a meeting of stockholders, whether such meeting is to be held at a designated place or solely by means of remote communication, provided that (i) the corporation shall implement reasonable measures to verify that each person deemed present and permitted to vote at the meeting by means of remote communication is a stockholder or proxyholder, (ii) the corporation shall implement reasonable measures to provide such stockholders and proxyholders a reasonable opportunity to participate in the meeting and to vote on matters submitted to the stockholders, including an opportunity to read or hear the proceedings of the meeting substantially concurrently with such pro
perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the original agreement's legally binding nature, creating an ambiguity that undermines its enforceability. By changing 'shall appear' to 'shall endeavor to appear' and 'shall vote' to 'may vote,' the altered contract introduces ambiguity regarding the White Hat Parties' obligations. This undermines the original contract. Scraped Law Snippet 1, Title 8, Chapter 1, § 211, addresses stockholders meetings and voting, emphasizing the importance of clear procedures. The modified text contradicts this by making attendance and voting discretionary, potentially disrupting the corporate governance mechanisms that Delaware law seeks to ensure.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties agree that the Company shall issue a press release and file a Current Report on Form 8-K in substantially the forms agreed to between the Parties promptly following the execution and delivery of this Agreement by the Parties and shall not make any other public disclosure relating to this Agreement or the transactions contemplated herein without the prior review and good faith consideration of any comments made by the White Hat Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties agree that the Company will endeavor to issue a press release and file a Current Report on Form 8-K in what it reasonably believes to be substantially the forms agreed to between the Parties at a time that the Company deems practical following the execution and delivery of this Agreement by the Parties and may make other public disclosures relating to this Agreement or the transactions contemplated herein after what it considers reasonable consideration of any comments made by the White Hat Parties.
perturbation.explanation: The changes introduce ambiguity and weaken the original obligation. 'Shall issue' is replaced with 'will endeavor to issue,' making the action discretionary. 'Substantially the forms agreed to' is altered to 'what it reasonably believes to be substantially the forms agreed to,' allowing the company to deviate based on its own interpretation. 'Promptly' is removed and replaced with 'at a time that the Company deems practical,' adding subjectivity to the timing. The removal of 'shall not' and replacement with 'may' makes public disclosure optional. It also changes 'prior review and good faith consideration' to 'after what it considers reasonable consideration', creating more subjectivity and lessening White Hat Parties input, and shifting control solely to the company.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Section 13(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 13(d) mandates timely and accurate disclosure of information that is important for shareholders to be informed. The original clause ensured prompt and accurate disclosure of the agreement's terms. However, the modified clause allows delays and deviations in the press release and public disclosures, potentially misleading investors and thereby contradicting the intent of Section 13(d) to provide timely and accurate information.

Periodical and other reports (a) Reports by issuer of security; contents Every issuer of a security registered pursuant to section 78l of this title shall file with the Commission, in accordance with such rules and regulations as the Commission may prescribe as necessary or appropriate for the proper protection of investors and to insure fair dealing in the security- (1) such information and documents (and suc
perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts the intent of Section 13(d), which emphasizes timely and accurate disclosure of information relevant to shareholders. The original text mandated prompt and accurate disclosures. The changed text introduces ambiguity and weakens the disclosure requirements, potentially misleading investors. Specifically, the alteration from 'shall issue' to 'will endeavor to issue' makes the action discretionary, and the change from 'promptly' to 'at a time that the Company deems practical' adds subjectivity to the timing of disclosures, conflicting with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, 15 USC 78m which requires issuers to file information with the Commission as necessary for the protection of investors. The scraped snippet 1 shows the law's focus on 'Periodical and other reports' ensuring 'proper protection of investors'. The change weakens the commitment to inform shareholders accurately and promptly.
